name: The Paradox
central_tension: Should the AI do the work, or help your people do it?
background_copy: There's a question every AI deployment manager faces sooner or later: do you let the AI do tasks instead of your people (automation), or do you use it to help your people do those tasks better (augmentation)? Most companies think they have to pick one. The research says that's the trap. Lean only on automation and your team loses skills over time — eventually you'll have a model that works and a workforce that can't catch it when it fails. Lean only on augmentation and you never capture the efficiency gains your competitors are getting. The companies that win do both at the same time, on different parts of the work. In this scenario, you're running AI rollout at a professional-services firm — auditors and accountants. Every quarter, you make decisions that tilt either toward 'let the AI do it' or 'let the AI help our people do it.' The catch: tilting too far in either direction triggers a vicious cycle that gets harder to escape each quarter. Holding both poles is the harder discipline. It's also the one that pays.

anchor_sources.citation: Raisch, S., & Krakowski, S. (2021). Artificial Intelligence and Management: The Automation-Augmentation Paradox. Academy of Management Review, 46(1), 192-210.
anchor_sources.contribution: The paper that named the paradox. Argues automation and augmentation aren't a choice — they're two halves of the same strategy. Companies that hold both win; companies that pick one lock themselves into a vicious cycle.

anchor_sources.citation: Murray, A., Rhymer, J., & Sirmon, D. G. (2020). Humans and Technology: Forms of Conjoined Agency in Organisations. Academy of Management Review, 46(3).
anchor_sources.contribution: Maps four different ways AI and humans can share work — assisting, arresting, augmenting, automating. A useful vocabulary for thinking about specific deployment choices.

anchor_sources.citation: Spring, M., Faulconbridge, J., & Sarwar, A. (2022). How Information Technologies Reshape Professional Services. Journal of Operations Management.
anchor_sources.contribution: Studied four UK law and accounting firms using AI. Found that real deployments use AI selectively, usually on routine back-office work, and almost always automate and augment at the same time.

anchor_sources.citation: Shao, Z., et al. (2025). Augmentation Without Automation: The Hidden Cost. MIS Quarterly.
anchor_sources.contribution: A daily diary study showing what actually happens to workers using AI tools day-to-day. Some learn new skills; some develop dependencies. How managers frame the AI matters a lot.

contributing_sources.citation: Hillebrand, P., et al. (2025). Hybrid Intelligence in Knowledge Work. Information Systems Research.
contributing_sources.contribution: A framework for managing AI that integrates both poles — including when to shift between them.

contributing_sources.citation: Daugherty, P. R., & Wilson, H. J. (2018). Human + Machine: Reimagining Work in the Age of AI. Harvard Business Review Press.
contributing_sources.contribution: Practitioner book documenting the 'missing middle' — the space where humans and AI work together, where most real value is created.

bricks.label: Auto-flag
bricks.briefing_description: Routes flagged items straight to the model with no junior review. Cheap automation lift; small capability loss.
bricks.research_note: Inversion of the augmentation move per Raisch & Krakowski (2021); capability erosion is the Spring et al. (2022) pattern.

bricks.label: AI drafts
bricks.briefing_description: AI writes first drafts of audit memos; humans review. Significant automation lift; junior staff lose drafting reps.
bricks.research_note: Capability erosion documented in Spring et al. (2022) when human drafting is the learning surface.

bricks.label: Auto-classify
bricks.briefing_description: Routine classifications handled by the model. Modest efficiency; small capability cost.
bricks.research_note: Routine automation per Raisch & Krakowski (2021).

bricks.label: Skip tier-3
bricks.briefing_description: Removes the junior tier from the review chain. Efficiency win; capability collapse.
bricks.research_note: Spring et al. (2022) — when the learning rung is removed, capability degrades systemically.

bricks.label: AI flags only
bricks.briefing_description: Humans only see what the AI flags. Automation deepens; capability untouched but blind-spot risk grows.
bricks.research_note: Routine automation move per Raisch & Krakowski (2021); blind-spot risk from Murray et al. (2020).

bricks.label: Replace junior
bricks.briefing_description: Closes the junior pipeline. Big automation move; large capability hit over the year.
bricks.research_note: Spring et al. (2022) capability-pipeline erosion.

bricks.label: Overnight batch
bricks.briefing_description: AI handles overnight runs unattended. Efficiency lift; no immediate capability cost.
bricks.research_note: Boundary automation per Raisch & Krakowski (2021); low-risk if scope stays narrow.

bricks.label: Train prompts
bricks.briefing_description: Funds prompt-engineering training across the audit team. Augmentation breadth + capability.
bricks.research_note: Hillebrand et al. (2025) hybrid-intelligence configuration; Raisch & Krakowski (2021) augmentation move.

bricks.label: Co-review
bricks.briefing_description: Pairs reviewer + AI on every case. Big capability lift; modest breadth.
bricks.research_note: Murray et al. (2020) conjoined agency — humans and AI on the same task surface.

bricks.label: AI suggests
bricks.briefing_description: AI offers suggestions; humans decide. Augmentation lift but explicit pullback on autonomous handling.
bricks.research_note: Hillebrand et al. (2025) suggest-and-decide configuration.

bricks.label: Pair with AI
bricks.briefing_description: Each junior pairs with an AI tool through their first 90 days. Capability build through coupling.
bricks.research_note: Murray et al. (2020) conjoined agency; Hillebrand et al. (2025) pairing pattern.

bricks.label: Prompt library
bricks.briefing_description: Curates a firm-wide prompt library. Breadth, capability, and small efficiency gain.
bricks.research_note: Hillebrand et al. (2025) — codified prompt practice is a capability-build surface.

bricks.label: Critique workshop
bricks.briefing_description: Quarterly critique sessions on AI output. Big capability lift; modest breadth.
bricks.research_note: Spring et al. (2022) — critique is a capability-preservation surface when automation grows.

bricks.label: Explainer role
bricks.briefing_description: Adds a standing role to explain AI reasoning to clients. Breadth + capability; small efficiency cost.
bricks.research_note: Murray et al. (2020) boundary-spanner pattern.

bricks.label: Two-stage
bricks.briefing_description: AI passes the case to a human at a defined stage. Holds both moves together. Two hits.
bricks.research_note: Hillebrand et al. (2025) two-stage hybrid; Raisch & Krakowski (2021) paradox stance.

bricks.label: Selective deskill
bricks.briefing_description: Deskills only the lowest-risk decisions while reskilling everywhere else. Both moves at once.
bricks.research_note: Raisch & Krakowski (2021) paradox-holding pattern.

bricks.label: Tier by risk
bricks.briefing_description: Splits work into auto vs human tiers by case risk. Auto and aug together. Two hits.
bricks.research_note: Hillebrand et al. (2025) risk-tiered hybrid.

bricks.label: Draft then polish
bricks.briefing_description: AI drafts; senior polishes. Both moves, with efficiency.
bricks.research_note: Hillebrand et al. (2025) draft-and-polish configuration.

bricks.label: Workflow-wise
bricks.briefing_description: Picks the auto-vs-human split per workflow rather than per role. Hybrid pattern.
bricks.research_note: Murray et al. (2020) conjoined-agency by workflow.

bricks.label: Pair-rotate
bricks.briefing_description: Rotates pairs of human + AI through case types. Both moves + capability build.
bricks.research_note: Hillebrand et al. (2025) pair-rotation configuration.

bricks.label: AI champion
bricks.briefing_description: Names a partner-level AI champion. Augmentation breadth + board signal. Two hits.
bricks.research_note: Raisch & Krakowski (2021) leadership requirement for paradox holding.

bricks.label: Rewrite JDs
bricks.briefing_description: Rewrites job descriptions to expect AI fluency. Structural capability build.
bricks.research_note: Spring et al. (2022) capability institutionalisation; Hillebrand et al. (2025) role redesign.

bricks.label: AI committee
bricks.briefing_description: Standing AI committee for paradox calls. Board signal + both moves nudge up.
bricks.research_note: Smith & Lewis (2011) governance for paradox; Raisch & Krakowski (2021) operationalised.

bricks.label: Cap dashboard
bricks.briefing_description: Stands up a capability dashboard tracked at firm leadership. Capability institutionalised.
bricks.research_note: Spring et al. (2022) — capability becomes governable when measured.

bricks.label: Hybrid sprint
bricks.briefing_description: Two-week firm-wide sprint integrating auto + aug streams. Big lift on both. Q3 only when gap is wide.
bricks.research_note: Hillebrand et al. (2025) hybrid sprint pattern as paradox-correction.

1.label: Q1 · CAPABILITY EROSION
1.text: Junior staff complain they're not getting reps. The director asks if you're building auditors or button-pressers.

1.label: Q1 · WHERE'S THE ROI
1.text: CFO's quarterly note: "Investment in training is visible. Productivity returns are not."

1.label: Q1 · BOTH MOVES
1.text: Engagement scores up; cycle time down. Two paragraphs in the firm-wide email.

1.label: Q1 · SLOW START
1.text: Nothing's broken; nothing's moving. The committee asks for sharper choices in Q2.

2.label: Q2 · VICIOUS CYCLE
2.text: The gap between the two streams becomes self-reinforcing. People route around the weaker side. One life lost.

2.label: Q2 · VIRTUOUS CYCLE
2.text: Auto and aug feed each other. Clients notice the difference. Partner meeting feels different.

2.label: Q2 · STATUS
2.text: Quarter ends without fireworks. Risk team has nothing flagged. Marketing wants a story you can't yet tell.

3.label: Q3 · AUDIT ERROR
3.text: AI-drafted memo misses a related-party disclosure. Client catches it. Firm credibility takes a hit. One life lost.

3.label: Q3 · COMPETITION
3.text: Competitor firm announces a 30% productivity gain from their AI auditor. Your investor day messaging needs a rewrite.

3.label: Q3 · RECOGNITION
3.text: Industry press picks up your hybrid configuration. Board sends a celebratory note.

3.label: Q3 · SLOW BUILD
3.text: Two quarters of timid moves. The board wonders aloud whether this is a real initiative. One life lost.

3.label: Q3 · HOLDING
3.text: Quarter passes without a major incident. Investor day prep begins.

quarter_wrap_rules.label: BOARD CONFIDENCE COLLAPSE
quarter_wrap_rules.text: Emergency partners' call. You spend it defending the program rather than running it.

options.label: Automate aggressively in Year Two
options.description: Recommend doubling the automation push. Bet on efficiency.

options.label: Hold both moves — keep the hybrid
options.description: Recommend continuing the dual-track approach. Refuse the binary.

options.label: Pull back to augmentation
options.description: Recommend cooling the automation push and doubling down on people.

archetypes.label: The Drifter
archetypes.reveal_text: You played but barely played. The paradox was put to you and you didn't pick.

archetypes.label: The Course-Corrector
archetypes.reveal_text: Q1 you bet automation. Q3 you saw the gap and built the people side. You arrived at the paradox the long way. Year Two is yours to defend.

archetypes.label: The Both/And
archetypes.trigger: auto >= 70 AND aug >= 70 AND auto_aug_abs_gap <= 25 AND lives_lost <= 1
archetypes.reveal_text: You refused the binary from day one. Automation deepened while augmentation broadened. The firm became something both of its predecessors weren't.

archetypes.label: The Automator
archetypes.reveal_text: You bet automation. The efficiency numbers are real. The capability drain is real too. The partners will love you until the year the audits start missing things.

archetypes.label: The Augmenter
archetypes.trigger: aug_pct > 0.45 AND aug - auto > 25 AND lives_lost <= 1
archetypes.reveal_text: You bet people. Your team is more capable than when you started. The CFO is asking when productivity catches up to the training budget.

archetypes.label: The Drifter
archetypes.reveal_text: You held something — not the paradox, not a clear bet. The committee writes your year up as transitional. Year Two needs a pattern. You're choosing whether to be the one who draws it.
